title: CL-03 Reverse-Engineered Sensitivity Analysis
method: Reverse engineering from known events + dimensional analysis. Instead of asking 'can K_eff predict X?', we ask: 'at known events, what was K_eff doing before, during, and after?' Three domains tested: Finance (1535 daily), EMBER (26 yearly), Backblaze (112 weekly).

finding_1_keff_leads_tv_at_covid.finding: K_eff departed >2σ from baseline ONE DAY before TV did at the COVID event
finding_1_keff_leads_tv_at_covid.caveat: Single event — one day of lead is suggestive but not statistically robust. However, the direction is consistent with the deceptive drift hypothesis: complexity dropped before velocity spiked.
finding_1_keff_leads_tv_at_covid.significance: This is the first empirical evidence that K_eff can move before TV in real data. Even one day of lead at a major structural event is non-trivial.

finding_2_keff_drops_before_tv_spikes.finding: K_eff drops an average of -0.53 in the 10 days before the 20 largest TV spikes. This is statistically significant (t=-3.10, p=0.002).
finding_2_keff_drops_before_tv_spikes.interpretation: Before major structural velocity events (TV spikes), K_eff is already declining. This is the deceptive drift pattern: concentration increases silently, then the system snaps to a new state (TV spike). K_eff sees the buildup; TV sees the release.
finding_2_keff_drops_before_tv_spikes.significance: STRONGEST finding in this analysis. Statistical significance at p=0.002 across 20 independent events over 6 years. This is the first real-data evidence that CL-03 carries forward-looking information relative to CL-02.

finding_3_deceptive_drift_predicts_futur.finding: When deceptive drift is occurring (K_eff falling, TV calm), future TV is significantly elevated at 10-day and 20-day horizons.

detail.definition: K_eff 20-day slope in bottom 10% AND TV below median

finding_3_deceptive_drift_predicts_futur.interpretation: Deceptive drift (K_eff declining while TV stays calm) statistically predicts elevated TV 10-20 days later. The signal strengthens with horizon: not significant at 5 days, significant at 10, highly significant at 20. This suggests a structural concentration process that builds over ~2-4 weeks before manifesting as velocity.
finding_3_deceptive_drift_predicts_futur.significance: This is the deceptive drift hypothesis tested empirically for the first time. The prediction isn't of a specific event, but of elevated structural turbulence — which is exactly what CL-03 was designed to detect.

finding_4_keff_and_tv_carry_independent_.finding: K_eff and TV are largely uncorrelated (r=-0.08 at level, r=-0.06 at changes), but |ΔK_eff| correlates with TV level at r=+0.59.
finding_4_keff_and_tv_carry_independent_.interpretation: K_eff and TV measure different things — as designed. But the MAGNITUDE of K_eff changes correlates moderately with TV level. When the system is in high-TV (turbulent) periods, K_eff fluctuates more. This is consistent: structural turbulence creates both velocity and complexity variation. The key point is that K_eff's DIRECTION carries information that TV's magnitude does not — downward K_eff predicts future upward TV.
finding_4_keff_and_tv_carry_independent_.significance: Confirms CL-03 is not redundant with CL-02. They share some variance (turbulent periods), but their directional information is independent.

finding_5_ember_keff_already_moving_befo.finding: At all three major events (Fukushima 2011, COVID 2020, Ukraine 2022), K_eff was already rising the year before.
finding_5_ember_keff_already_moving_befo.interpretation: EMBER World K_eff has been monotonically rising since 2000 (from 4.25 to 5.97) — driven by the global energy transition adding solar, wind, and other renewables to the mix. The events ACCELERATE an existing trend rather than reversing it. K_eff was rising before Fukushima because renewables were growing; Fukushima accelerated the trend (nuclear → gas/renewables substitution). This is a different pattern than finance: K_eff trend reflects long-term policy, not short-term structural stress.
finding_5_ember_keff_already_moving_befo.caveat: Annual resolution is too coarse for lead/lag analysis of discrete events. The EMBER finding confirms structural dynamics but cannot resolve whether K_eff leads or lags within-year.

finding_6_backblaze_orthogonality_confir.finding: K_eff vs failure rate: r=-0.056, p=0.56. No significant correlation at any lag from -4 to +4 weeks.
finding_6_backblaze_orthogonality_confir.interpretation: Confirms T5a at weekly resolution over 2 years. Fleet composition complexity has no relationship to failure rate at any temporal offset. This is the orthogonality baseline — K_eff measures structure, failure rate measures reliability. Different information domains.
finding_6_backblaze_orthogonality_confir.note: The Backblaze fleet is structurally stable (8.12% K_eff variation over 2 years). A more structurally dynamic fleet might show different results.

finding_7_granger_tests_null.finding: Formal Granger causality tests (5-lag VAR) show neither K_eff→TV nor TV→K_eff at conventional significance.
finding_7_granger_tests_null.interpretation: The linear Granger test finds no predictive relationship in either direction. This seems to contradict Finding 2 (K_eff drops before TV spikes). The resolution: Granger tests for linear, unconditional predictability across the entire time series. K_eff's forward signal is CONDITIONAL — it only activates during deceptive drift episodes, not continuously. The signal is nonlinear and regime-dependent.
finding_7_granger_tests_null.significance: This is an important methodological finding: standard VAR/Granger methods won't detect CL-03's signal because the signal is conditional on regime (drift periods only) and nonlinear (directional drop → future spike). Event-triggered analysis and conditional tests are the appropriate methods.

sensitivity_floor.finding: A 10% sector weight shift produces a 2σ K_eff change in finance. Smaller shifts (1-5%) are below noise floor.
sensitivity_floor.implication: CL-03 resolves sector-level shifts of ≥5% (1σ) to ≥10% (2σ). Sub-5% shifts are below the noise floor in a 9-sector finance carrier. This is consistent with T4b: 5% cross-sector transfer was detectable at 2.21× TV baseline.

response_dynamics.finding: K_eff changes are anti-correlated at lag-1 (r=-0.32), meaning daily K_eff is mean-reverting. Perturbations decay within 1-2 days.
response_dynamics.implication: K_eff has a natural restoring force — random daily fluctuations are corrected next-day. Only sustained, multi-day concentration trends (like deceptive drift) accumulate into significant signal. This is why the deceptive drift test uses a 20-day slope: single-day K_eff moves are noise.

dimensional_decomposition.finding: Technology weight is the dominant anti-driver of K_eff (r=-0.73 level, r=-0.59 change). Financial Services is the strongest positive driver (r=+0.67 level, r=+0.53 change).
dimensional_decomposition.interpretation: K_eff drops when Technology (the largest sector at 33.6%) concentrates further. K_eff rises when Financial Services and other mid-weight sectors gain share. This is mathematically expected: K_eff (Shannon exponential) increases with evenness and decreases with concentration. The top sector drives K_eff mechanically. But the RATE of this concentration provides the deceptive drift signal.

information_independence.finding: K_eff and TV carry largely independent information (r=-0.08), but |ΔK_eff| correlates with TV at r=+0.59.
information_independence.implication: CL-03 is NOT redundant with CL-02. They share turbulence-period variance, but K_eff direction provides unique information. Outcome (C) from the previous assessment — that K_eff adds nothing beyond TV — is ruled out.

predictability.finding: K_eff is moderately predictable from its own history (23.3% entropy reduction from previous day).
predictability.implication: K_eff carries both predictable structure (23%) and innovation (77%). The predictable component is the mean-reversion at daily scale. The innovation component is where new structural information enters the system.

cl03_status_update.prior_status: ADD* — S-curve confirmed by simulation, real-data gate outstanding
this_analysis_provides: First real-data evidence of K_eff leading TV (1 day at COVID, p=suggestive)
First statistical evidence of K_eff dropping before TV spikes (p=0.002 across 20 events)
First empirical test of deceptive drift prediction (p=0.005 at 10d, p<0.001 at 20d)
Confirmation that K_eff and TV carry independent information (r=-0.08)
Ruling out of outcome (C): K_eff is NOT redundant with TV
cl03_status_update.does_this_close_the_gate: NOT YET — but it significantly narrows the gap. The deceptive drift finding (Finding 3) is the strongest candidate for gate closure. What's still needed: (1) out-of-sample validation on held-out data, (2) replication in a second domain with sufficient temporal resolution, (3) demonstration that the signal is actionable (not just statistically significant). The finance dataset should be split: train on 2020-2022, test on 2023-2026.
cl03_status_update.recommended_next_step: Split-sample validation: use first 3 years to calibrate deceptive drift threshold, test on last 3 years. If the 10-20 day TV elevation persists out-of-sample, CL-03 moves from ADD* to ADD with empirical support.

revised_assessment_of_three_outcomes.outcome_A_prediction_confirmed: PARTIALLY SUPPORTED — conditional, nonlinear prediction signal exists (deceptive drift → elevated TV). Not universal, not linear, not Granger-detectable. But real.
revised_assessment_of_three_outcomes.outcome_B_concurrent_only: PARTIALLY CONTRADICTED — K_eff carries some forward-looking information, not just concurrent. The lead is small (1-20 days in finance) but statistically significant.
revised_assessment_of_three_outcomes.outcome_C_redundant_with_tv: RULED OUT — K_eff and TV correlation is r=-0.08. They measure different aspects of structure.

corpus_trail.triggered_by: Peter's suggestion to reverse-engineer CL-03 capabilities from known events
updates_needed: APP-001 should incorporate deceptive drift as conditional prediction capability
CAL-001 CL-03 status should reference this analysis
